snip_output.equation: <math display="block">f:\mathbf{R}^n\to\mathbf{R}</math> be a smooth function. Define <math display="block">S:=\{x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\in\mathbf{R}^n:f(x)=0\}.</math> Assume that <math display="block">\nabla f(x)\neq 0 \quad \text{ for every } x\in S.</math> Then for every point <math>p\in S</math>, there exists an index <math>j\in \{1,\dots,n\}</math>, an open neighborhood <math>W\subset\mathbf{R}^n</math> of <math>p</math>, an open set <math>U\subset\mathbf{R}^{n-1}</math>, and a differentiable function <math display="block">g:U\to\mathbf{R}</math> such that, after possibly reordering the coordinates, the set <math>S\cap W</math> can be written in the form <math display="block">S\cap W=\Big\{(x_1,\dots,x_{j-1},g(x_1,\dots,x_{j-1},x_{j+1},\dots,x_n),\,x_{j+1},\dots,x_n):(x_1,\dots,x_{j-1},x_{j+1},\dots,x_n)\in U\Big\}.</math> In other words, near every point of <math>S</math>, the hypersurface <math>S</math> is locally the graph of a differentiable function of <math>n-1</math> variables.
